// Theme and CSS custom properties.
package vogui

// Theme defines the application's design tokens.
// These are emitted as CSS custom properties (--vo-primary, etc.).
type Theme struct {
	// Core semantic colors
	Primary           string
	PrimaryForeground string
	Secondary           string
	SecondaryForeground string
	Success             string
	SuccessForeground   string
	Danger              string
	DangerForeground    string
	Warning             string
	WarningForeground   string
	Info                string
	InfoForeground      string

	// Surface colors
	Background string
	Surface    string
	Card            string
	CardForeground  string
	Popover            string
	PopoverForeground  string
	Muted              string

	// Interactive colors
	Accent           string
	AccentForeground string
	Ring             string
	InputBorder      string

	// Text colors
	Text       string
	TextMuted  string

	// Border
	Border     string

	// Layout tokens
	Radius     string
	RadiusLg   string
	RadiusSm   string
	FontFamily string

// themeToCSSVars converts the current theme to a map of CSS custom property
// names to values, suitable for encoding in the binary render protocol.
// Returns nil if no theme is set.
func themeToCSSVars() map[string]string {
	if currentTheme == nil {
		return nil
	}
	t := currentTheme
	m := map[string]string{}
	set := func(k string, v string) {
		if v != "" {
			m[k] = v
		}
	}
	set("--vo-primary", t.Primary)
	set("--vo-primary-foreground", t.PrimaryForeground)
	set("--vo-secondary", t.Secondary)
	set("--vo-secondary-foreground", t.SecondaryForeground)

	set("--vo-background", t.Background)
	set("--vo-surface", t.Surface)
	set("--vo-card", t.Card)
	set("--vo-card-foreground", t.CardForeground)
	set("--vo-popover", t.Popover)
	set("--vo-popover-foreground", t.PopoverForeground)
	set("--vo-muted", t.Muted)
	set("--vo-accent", t.Accent)
	set("--vo-accent-foreground", t.AccentForeground)
	set("--vo-ring", t.Ring)
	set("--vo-input-border", t.InputBorder)

	set("--vo-radius-lg", t.RadiusLg)
	set("--vo-radius-sm", t.RadiusSm)
	set("--vo-font-family", t.FontFamily)
	return m
}

package main

import (
	"encoding/binary"
	"github.com/vo-lang/vopack"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

func assert(cond bool, msg string) {
	if !cond {
		panic("FAIL: " + msg)
	}
}

func mustReadFile(path string) []byte {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
	assert(err == nil, "read file: "+path)
	return data
}

func contains(s, needle string) bool {
	if needle == "" {
		return true
	}
	if len(needle) > len(s) {
		return false
	}
	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(needle); i++ {
		if s[i:i+len(needle)] == needle {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

func entryTableOffset(data []byte) int {
	footerOff := len(data) - 32
	return int(binary.LittleEndian.Uint64(data[footerOff+16 : footerOff+24]))
}

func entryOffset(data []byte, entryIndex int, fieldOffset int) int {
	return entryTableOffset(data) + entryIndex*48 + fieldOffset
}

func finalLZ4LiteralLen(src []byte) int {
	sPos := 0
	lastLitLen := 0
	for sPos < len(src) {
		token := src[sPos]
		sPos++

		litLen := int(token >> 4)
		if litLen == 15 {
			for {
				extra := src[sPos]
				sPos++
				litLen += int(extra)
				if extra != 255 {
					break
				}
			}
		}

		sPos += litLen
		lastLitLen = litLen
		if sPos >= len(src) {
			return lastLitLen
		}

		sPos += 2
		matchLen := int(token&0x0F) + 4
		if matchLen == 19 {
			for {
				extra := src[sPos]
				sPos++
				matchLen += int(extra)
				if extra != 255 {
					break
				}
			}
		}
	}
	return lastLitLen
}

func main() {
	tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "vopack-test-")
	assert(err == nil, "create temp dir")
	defer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)

	testBasicRoundTrip(tmpDir)
	testCompression(tmpDir)

}

func testBasicRoundTrip(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "basic.vpak"})

	// Write
	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create basic archive")

	err = w.Add("hello.txt", []byte("Hello, vopack!"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add hello.txt")

	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close writer")

	// Read
	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open basic archive")

	assert(r.Has("hello.txt"), "has hello.txt")
	assert(!r.Has("nope.txt"), "has nope.txt should be false")

	data, err := r.Read("hello.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "read hello.txt")
	assert(string(data) == "Hello, vopack!", "content mismatch: "+string(data))

	size, err := r.Size("hello.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "size hello.txt")
	assert(size == 14, "size should be 14")

	assert(r.Len() == 1, "len should be 1")

	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close reader")
}

func testCompression(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "compressed.vpak"})

	// Create repetitive data that compresses well
	data := make([]byte, 4096)
	for i := 0; i < len(data); i++ {
		data[i] = byte(i % 10) + 'A'
	}

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create compressed archive")

	err = w.Add("data.bin", data, vopack.EntryOpts{Compression: vopack.CompressLZ4})
	assert(err == nil, "add compressed entry")

	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close compressed writer")

	// Read back
	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open compressed archive")

	result, err := r.Read("data.bin")
	assert(err == nil, "read compressed data")
	assert(len(result) == 4096, "decompressed size mismatch")

	// Verify content
	for i := 0; i < len(data); i++ {
		assert(result[i] == data[i], "content byte mismatch at index")
	}

	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close compressed reader")
}

func testMultipleEntries(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "multi.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create multi archive")

	err = w.Add("a.txt", []byte("aaa"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add a.txt")
	err = w.Add("b.txt", []byte("bbb"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add b.txt")
	err = w.Add("c.txt", []byte("ccc"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add c.txt")

	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close multi writer")

	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open multi archive")

	assert(r.Len() == 3, "multi len should be 3")

	da, err := r.Read("a.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "read a.txt")
	db, err := r.Read("b.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "read b.txt")
	dc, err := r.Read("c.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "read c.txt")
	assert(string(da) == "aaa", "a content")
	assert(string(db) == "bbb", "b content")
	assert(string(dc) == "ccc", "c content")

	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close multi reader")
}

func testRejectDuplicatePaths(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "duplicate.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create duplicate archive")

	err = w.Add("dup.txt", []byte("first"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add first duplicate candidate")

	err = w.Add("dup.txt", []byte("second"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err != nil, "duplicate path should fail")

	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close duplicate archive")

	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open duplicate archive")
	assert(r.Len() == 1, "duplicate archive should contain exactly one entry")

	data, err := r.Read("dup.txt")
	assert(err == nil, "read surviving duplicate entry")
	assert(string(data) == "first", "duplicate archive should retain first entry")

	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close duplicate archive reader")
}

	assert(err == nil, "close corrupt header archive")

	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
	assert(err == nil, "read corrupt header archive")
	data[8] = 0
	err = os.WriteFile(path, data, 0644)

func testRejectUncompressedSizeMismatch(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "size-mismatch.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create size mismatch archive")
	err = w.Add("a.txt", []byte("abc"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add size mismatch entry")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close size mismatch archive")

	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
	assert(err == nil, "read size mismatch archive")
	footerOff := len(data) - 32
	entryTableOff := int(binary.LittleEndian.Uint64(data[footerOff+16 : footerOff+24]))
	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(data[entryTableOff+16:], 99)
	err = os.WriteFile(path, data, 0644)
	assert(err == nil, "rewrite size mismatch archive")

	_, err = vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err != nil, "uncompressed size mismatch should fail")

func testListPrefix(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "list.vpak"})

func testManifest(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "manifest.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create manifest archive")
	w.SetManifestInfo("test-pack", "1.2.3")
	srcPath := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "road.png"})
	err = os.WriteFile(srcPath, []byte("road-texture"), 0644)
	assert(err == nil, "write manifest source")
	err = w.AddFile("textures/road.png", srcPath, vopack.EntryOpts{Type: "texture"})
	assert(err == nil, "add manifest texture")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close manifest archive")

	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open manifest archive")
	assert(r.Len() == 1, "manifest metadata should not count as resource")
	assert(!r.Has(vopack.ManifestPath), "manifest metadata should not be a public resource")
	manifest, err := r.Manifest()
	assert(err == nil, "read manifest")
	assert(manifest.PackName == "test-pack", "manifest pack name")
	assert(manifest.PackVersion == "1.2.3", "manifest pack version")
	assert(len(manifest.Assets) == 1, "manifest asset count")
	assert(manifest.Assets[0].Path == "textures/road.png", "manifest asset path")
	assert(manifest.Assets[0].Type == "texture", "manifest asset type")
	assert(manifest.Assets[0].ContentHash != "", "manifest asset hash")
	assert(manifest.Assets[0].SourcePath != "", "manifest source path")
	_, err = r.Read(vopack.ManifestPath)
	assert(err != nil, "manifest should not read as normal resource")
	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close manifest reader")
}

func testManifestDependencies(tmpDir string) {

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)

	err = w.Add("tracks/main/track.json", []byte("{}"), vopack.EntryOpts{Type: "track", Dependencies: []string{"tracks/main/road.glb", "tracks/main/road.png"}})
	assert(err == nil, "add track with deps")
	err = w.Add("tracks/main/road.glb", []byte("road"), vopack.EntryOpts{Type: "model", Dependencies: []string{"tracks/main/road.png"}})
	assert(err == nil, "add road model with deps")
	err = w.Add("tracks/main/road.png", []byte("texture"), vopack.EntryOpts{Type: "texture"})
	assert(err == nil, "add road texture")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close manifest deps archive")

	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open manifest deps archive")
	err = r.VerifyManifestDependencies("tracks/main/track.json")
	assert(err == nil, "verify manifest deps")
	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close manifest deps reader")

	broken := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "manifest-deps-broken.vpak"})
	w, err = vopack.Create(broken)
	assert(err == nil, "create broken manifest deps archive")
	err = w.Add("tracks/main/track.json", []byte("{}"), vopack.EntryOpts{Type: "track", Dependencies: []string{"tracks/main/missing.glb"}})
	assert(err == nil, "add broken track")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close broken manifest deps archive")
	r, err = vopack.Open(broken)
	assert(err == nil, "open broken manifest deps archive")
	err = r.VerifyManifestDependencies("tracks/main/track.json")
	assert(err != nil, "missing manifest dependency should fail")
	assert(contains(err.Error(), "tracks/main/track.json") && contains(err.Error(), "tracks/main/missing.glb"), "dependency error includes requester and missing path")
	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close broken manifest deps reader")
}

func testMountOverlay(tmpDir string) {
	basePath := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "base.vpak"})
	patchPath := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "patch.vpak"})

	// Base archive
	w, err := vopack.Create(basePath)
	assert(err == nil, "create base archive")
	err = w.Add("hero.png", []byte("base-hero"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add base hero")
	err = w.Add("enemy.png", []byte("base-enemy"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add base enemy")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close base archive")

	// Patch archive overrides hero
	w, err = vopack.Create(patchPath)
	assert(err == nil, "create patch archive")
	err = w.Add("hero.png", []byte("patched-hero"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add patch hero")
	err = w.Add("new-item.png", []byte("new-item"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add patch item")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close patch archive")

	// Mount with priority
	m := vopack.NewMountSet()
	err = m.Mount(basePath, 0)
	assert(err == nil, "mount base")
	err = m.Mount(patchPath, 10)
	assert(err == nil, "mount patch")

	// Patch should override hero
	hero, err := m.Read("hero.png")
	assert(err == nil, "read hero from mount")
	assert(string(hero) == "patched-hero", "hero should be patched")

	// Base enemy still accessible
	enemy, err := m.Read("enemy.png")
	assert(err == nil, "read enemy from mount")
	assert(string(enemy) == "base-enemy", "enemy should be base")

	// New item from patch
	item, err := m.Read("new-item.png")
	assert(err == nil, "read new-item from mount")
	assert(string(item) == "new-item", "new-item content")

	// List should be merged and deduplicated
	all := m.List("")
	assert(len(all) == 3, "mount list should be 3 (hero, enemy, new-item)")

	assert(m.Has("hero.png"), "mount has hero")
	assert(!m.Has("nope.png"), "mount has nope should be false")

	err = m.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close mount set")
}

func testEmptyArchive(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "empty.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create empty archive")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close empty writer")

	r, err := vopack.Open(path)
	assert(err == nil, "open empty archive")
	assert(r.Len() == 0, "empty archive len should be 0")
	assert(!r.Has("anything"), "empty has should be false")

	all := r.List("")
	assert(len(all) == 0, "empty list should be 0")

	err = r.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close empty archive reader")
}

func testAlignmentLayout(tmpDir string) {
	path := filepath.Join([]string{tmpDir, "alignment.vpak"})

	w, err := vopack.Create(path)
	assert(err == nil, "create alignment archive")
	err = w.Add("a.bin", []byte("abc"), vopack.EntryOpts{})
	assert(err == nil, "add a.bin")
	err = w.Add("b.bin", []byte("payload"), vopack.EntryOpts{Alignment: 64})
	assert(err == nil, "add b.bin")
	err = w.Close()
	assert(err == nil, "close alignment archive")

	data := mustReadFile(path)
	bDataOff := binary.LittleEndian.Uint64(data[entryOffset(data, 1, 8):])
	assert(bDataOff%64 == 0, "aligned entry data offset should be 64-byte aligned")
}

func testLZ4Compress() {
	// Test round-trip on various data patterns

	// 1. Empty
	c := vopack.Compress([]byte{})
	assert(len(c) == 0, "compress empty")
	d, err := vopack.Decompress(c, 0)
	assert(err == nil, "decompress empty")
	assert(len(d) == 0, "decompress empty len")

	// 2. Short data (< 13 bytes, cannot compress smaller)
	short := []byte("Hello!")
	c = vopack.Compress(short)
	assert(c == nil, "short data should not compress")

	// 3. Highly repetitive data
	rep := make([]byte, 10000)
	for i := 0; i < len(rep); i++ {
		rep[i] = byte(i % 4)
	}
	c = vopack.Compress(rep)
	assert(c != nil, "repetitive should compress")
	assert(len(c) < len(rep), "compressed should be smaller")
	d, err = vopack.Decompress(c, len(rep))
	assert(err == nil, "decompress repetitive")
	for i := 0; i < len(rep); i++ {
		assert(d[i] == rep[i], "repetitive byte mismatch")
	}

	// 4. Random-ish data (may not compress)
	rnd := make([]byte, 1000)
	for i := 0; i < len(rnd); i++ {
		rnd[i] = byte((i*7 + 13) % 256)
	}
	c = vopack.Compress(rnd)
	if c != nil {
		// If it did compress, verify round-trip
		d, err = vopack.Decompress(c, len(rnd))
		assert(err == nil, "decompress random")
		for i := 0; i < len(rnd); i++ {
			assert(d[i] == rnd[i], "random byte mismatch")
		}
	}
	// c == nil means compression didn't help — that's expected for random data
}

func testLZ4StandardEnding() {
	rep := make([]byte, 4096)
	for i := 0; i < len(rep); i++ {
		rep[i] = byte(i % 4)
	}

	c := vopack.Compress(rep)
	assert(c != nil, "standard ending input should compress")
	assert(finalLZ4LiteralLen(c) >= 5, "compressed block should end with at least 5 trailing literals")

	d, err := vopack.Decompress(c, len(rep))
	assert(err == nil, "decompress standard ending block")
	for i := 0; i < len(rep); i++ {
		assert(d[i] == rep[i], "standard ending byte mismatch")
	}
}

func testLZ4RejectMalformedInput() {
	_, err := vopack.Decompress([]byte{0}, 0)
	assert(err != nil, "non-empty compressed data for empty output should fail")

	_, err = vopack.Decompress([]byte{0x1f, 'A', 0x01, 0x00, 0xff}, 275)
	assert(err != nil, "truncated match length should fail")
}

package main

import "github.com/vo-lang/voplay"

func testInputNormalization() {
	input := voplay.NewInputState()
	actions := voplay.NewActionMap(input)
	actions.Bind("left", voplay.KeyBinding("a"))
	actions.Bind("right", voplay.KeyBinding("d"))
	actions.Bind("up", voplay.KeyBinding("w"))
	actions.Bind("down", voplay.KeyBinding("s"))

	input.KeyDown("A")
	assert(actions.IsDown("left"), "uppercase A should match bound lowercase a")
	input.KeyUp("A")
	assert(actions.IsReleased("left"), "uppercase A release should match bound lowercase a")
	input.EndFrame()

	input.KeyDown("W")
	assert(actions.IsDown("up"), "uppercase W should match bound lowercase w")
	input.KeyUp("W")
	assert(actions.IsReleased("up"), "uppercase W release should match bound lowercase w")
	input.EndFrame()

	actions.Rebind("left", voplay.KeyBinding("KeyA"))
	actions.Rebind("up", voplay.KeyBinding("KeyW"))
	input.KeyDown("a")
	assert(actions.IsDown("left"), "single-letter a should match KeyA binding")
	input.KeyUp("a")
	assert(actions.IsReleased("left"), "single-letter a release should match KeyA binding")
	input.EndFrame()

	input.KeyDown("w")
	assert(actions.IsDown("up"), "single-letter w should match KeyW binding")
	assert(actions.Axis("down", "up") == 1, "normalized W should drive positive axis")
	input.KeyUp("w")
	assert(actions.IsReleased("up"), "single-letter w release should match KeyW binding")
}

package main

import "math"
import "github.com/vo-lang/voplay"
import "github.com/vo-lang/voplay/scene3d"

func testThirdPersonCameraRotationSemantics() {
	s := scene3d.New()
	target := s.Spawn(scene3d.EntityDesc{
		Position: voplay.Vec3{X: 1, Y: 2, Z: 3},
	})
	camCtl := scene3d.NewThirdPersonCamera(target)
	camCtl.PivotOffset = voplay.Vec3{Y: 1}
	camCtl.Distance = 4
	camCtl.MinDistance = 2
	camCtl.MaxDistance = 6
	camCtl.Smoothing = 0
	camCtl.CollisionEnabled = false
	camCtl.Yaw = 0
	camCtl.Pitch = 0
	camCtl.UpdateCamera(&s.Camera, s, 1.0/60.0)
	pivot := voplay.Vec3{X: 1, Y: 3, Z: 3}
	assertVec3Near(s.Camera.Target, pivot, 0.000001, "third person initial target")
	assertVec3Near(s.Camera.Position, voplay.Vec3{X: 1, Y: 3, Z: 7}, 0.000001, "third person initial position")

	target.SetRotation(voplay.QuatFromAxisAngle(voplay.Vec3{Y: 1}, math.Pi/2))
	camCtl.UpdateCamera(&s.Camera, s, 1.0/60.0)
	assertVec3Near(s.Camera.Position, voplay.Vec3{X: 1, Y: 3, Z: 7}, 0.000001, "third person ignores target rotation by default")

	camCtl.AlignToTargetYaw = true
	camCtl.UpdateCamera(&s.Camera, s, 1.0/60.0)
	assertVec3Near(s.Camera.Position, voplay.Vec3{X: 5, Y: 3, Z: 3}, 0.000001, "third person optional target yaw alignment")

	camCtl.AlignToTargetYaw = false
	target.SetRotation(voplay.QuatIdentity())
	camCtl.UpdateCamera(&s.Camera, s, 1.0/60.0)
	assertVec3Near(s.Camera.Position, voplay.Vec3{X: 1, Y: 3, Z: 7}, 0.000001, "third person returns to free orbit")

	s.Close()
}

package main

import "github.com/vo-lang/voplay"

func testPointerContactsAndTouchUI() {
	input := voplay.NewInputState()
	input.PointerDownID(7, 10, 20, 0)
	input.PointerDownID(8, 220, 60, 0)
	assert(input.HasPointerInput(), "pointer input is marked seen")
	assert(input.PointerDownContactCount() == 2, "two pointer contacts can be down together")
	assert(input.IsPointerDown(0), "shared pointer button stays down while contacts are down")
	assert(input.IsPointerContactPressed(7), "first contact pressed")
	assert(input.IsPointerContactPressed(8), "second contact pressed")
	contact, ok := input.PointerContact(7)
	assert(ok, "pointer contact lookup succeeds")
	assert(contact.ID == 7 && contact.Down, "pointer contact records id and down state")
	assertFloatNear(contact.X, 10, 0.000001, "pointer contact x")
	assertFloatNear(contact.Y, 20, 0.000001, "pointer contact y")
	button := voplay.ReadTouchButton(input, voplay.Rect{X: 200, Y: 40, W: 80, H: 80})
	assert(button.Down, "touch button reads down contact inside rect")
	assert(button.Pressed, "touch button reads pressed contact inside rect")
	stick := voplay.ReadTouchStick(input, voplay.Rect{X: 0, Y: 0, W: 140, H: 180}, voplay.Vec2{X: 40, Y: 40}, 50)
	assert(stick.Active, "touch stick activates from left contact")
	assert(stick.ID == 7, "touch stick keeps contact id")
	assertFloatNear(stick.Axis.X, -0.6, 0.000001, "touch stick axis x")
	assertFloatNear(stick.Axis.Y, -0.4, 0.000001, "touch stick axis y")
	input.EndFrame()
	assert(!input.IsPointerContactPressed(7), "contact pressed clears after end frame")
	input.PointerMoveID(7, 100, 40)
	stick = voplay.ReadTouchStick(input, voplay.Rect{X: 0, Y: 0, W: 140, H: 180}, voplay.Vec2{X: 40, Y: 40}, 50)
	assert(stick.Active, "touch stick remains active after move")
	assertFloatNear(stick.Axis.X, 1, 0.000001, "touch stick clamps axis x")
	assertFloatNear(stick.Axis.Y, 0, 0.000001, "touch stick clamps axis y")
	input.PointerUpID(8, 220, 60, 0)
	assert(input.IsPointerContactReleased(8), "second contact released")
	assert(input.IsPointerDown(0), "button remains down while first contact is still held")
	input.PointerUpID(7, 100, 40, 0)
	assert(!input.IsPointerDown(0), "button clears after all contacts release")
	input.EndFrame()
	assert(input.PointerContactCount() == 0, "released contacts are dropped after end frame")
}
